rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in the theory section to justify the core premise that shared hedonic consumption changes enjoyment, not just judgments. That premise is central to the citing paper's construct of shared consumption influence and is carried into its hypotheses and ABM design for movie life cycles and advertising effects. The citation is therefore more than background listing; it provides behavioral grounding for the paper's main conceptual setup.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Raghunathan and Corfman (2006) as core behavioral support that co-consumption affects hedonic enjoyment (including possible negative effects under incongruent reactions). It uses this foundation to build a market-level model where shared consumption influences diffusion patterns and advertising effectiveness in movies.
